neighborhoods.name: Lincoln Park / Lakeview
neighborhoods.description: North Side lakefront neighborhoods with the zoo, DePaul University, and vibrant dining scene
neighborhoods.character: Lakefront, zoo, university area, vibrant dining

housing.predominantType: Brownstones, condos, and vintage apartment buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Chicago. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Evanston
neighborhoods.description: Lakefront suburb with Northwestern University, diverse community, and excellent public transit
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Chicago's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Berwyn / Cicero
neighborhoods.description: Western suburbs with bungalow belt housing, diverse communities, and Blue Line access

housing.predominantType: Classic Chicago bungalows and modest houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Oak Park
neighborhoods.description: Historic suburb with Frank Lloyd Wright architecture, excellent schools, and diverse community
neighborhoods.character: Frank Lloyd Wright, excellent schools, diverse

housing.predominantType: Prairie-style homes, Victorians, and newer condos

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
